rca_details.title: WebKit Code Change Caused Unexpected Bundle Loading Failure
rca_details.symptom: The test assertion `expect(output).toContain('utilsBundle')` failed because the test runner's output did not contain the expected bundle name.
rca_details.root_cause: Code changes within the WebKit implementation had an unintended side effect on the project's build process, altering the module loading behavior of the 'playwright-test' package. This caused a performance test, designed to monitor bundle loading, to fail.

evidence.content: The test log shows a failure in 'perf.spec.ts' where the test runner's module load output was expected to contain 'utilsBundle' but did not.
The PR diff shows numerous code changes and refactoring within the 'src/webkit/' directory.
The failing test, 'perf.spec.ts', is an internal test for the Playwright test runner, not for the WebKit functionality that was modified.

rca_details.remediation: The code changes in the WebKit module should be reviewed for unintended side effects on the build and bundling process. The build configuration may need to be adjusted to isolate modules better. Alternatively, if the change in bundle loading is acceptable, the performance test should be updated to reflect the new behavior.

rca_details.title: E2E Tests Fail Due to Backend Compilation Error
rca_details.symptom: End-to-end tests fail because the browser cannot connect to the application server (`ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED`). The underlying cause is a compilation failure in the backend Go application, which prevents the server from starting.
rca_details.root_cause: The backend Go application fails to compile due to type errors in auto-generated code from the `ent` framework. This occurred because schema changes (adding new fields to `item.go`) were not correctly propagated by the code generation step, resulting in type mismatches (e.g., pointer vs. value types) in the committed code.

evidence.content: All Playwright tests failed with the same error: `page.goto: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED at http://localhost:7745/home`
The `Backend Server Tests / Go` job failed with typecheck errors during linting: `cannot use oldValue.LastInventoryAt (variable of type *'time'.Time) as 'time'.Time value in return statement`
The PR diff shows changes to the `ent` schema file `backend/internal/data/ent/schema/item.go`, adding new nillable fields.
The committed, auto-generated `ent` code (e.g., `mutation.go`) contains type mismatches corresponding to the new schema fields, which is the source of the compilation error.

rca_details.remediation: The auto-generated `ent` code is out of sync with the schema changes. The developer needs to run `ent generate ./internal/data/ent/schema` locally and commit the corrected files. This will resolve the backend compilation errors, allowing the application server to build and run for the E2E tests.
